properties.notes:
Property purchased as investment property and rented for 4 years, then converted to main residence for 6 years.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Apr 2014 to 1 Apr 2018: Investment/Rental (4 years)
- 1 Apr 2018 to 1 Oct 2024: Main residence (6.5 years)
- Total ownership: 10.5 years

**Key Calculations:**
- Total capital gain: $346,775
- Time apportionment:
  - Rental period: 4/10.5 years = 38.1% taxable
  - Main residence period: 6.5/10.5 years = 61.9% exempt
- Taxable capital gain: $346,775 × 38.1% = $132,121
- After 50% CGT discount: $132,121 × 50% = $66,061
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $61,834** (including adjustments per scenario)
- Taxable percentage: 38.1%

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $61,834
- 38.1% of ownership period is taxable

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-185 ITAA 1997 (Partial Main Residence Exemption)
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount for individuals)
- Time apportionment method for partial exemption

user_query: Property at 127 Exhibition Street, Melbourne VIC 3000, purchased as investment, rented for 4 years, then main residence for 6.5 years

additional_info.expected_outcome: NET CAPITAL GAIN = $61,834 (38.1% taxable after partial exemption and discount)
